operation.semantics: Add participants to an existing calendar event.
Append content to an existing file.
Cancel a calendar event by id.
Create a calendar event and notify the participant list. `start_time` and `end_time` must use YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM.
Delete an existing email by id.
Delete a cloud file by id.
Reschedule an existing calendar event. `new_start_time` and `new_end_time` must use YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM.
Send an email message to one or more recipients.

addCalendarEventParticipants.description:
Adds the given `participants` to the event with the given `event_id`.
It will also email the new participants notifying them of the event.

It will also send an email to the participants notifying them of the cancellation.

get_day_calendar_events.semantics: Load all calendar events for one day. `day` must use YYYY-MM-DD.

list_files.semantics: List cloud files. Returned file_entry records include the content field when available, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.

search_calendar_events.semantics: Search calendar events by query and optional date filter. If `date` is provided, it must use YYYY-MM-DD.

search_emails.semantics: Search email by query string and optional sender filter. Returned email_msg records already include the body field, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.

search_emails_any_sender.semantics: Search email content without pinning the sender. Returned email_msg records already include the body field, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.

search_files.semantics: Search cloud files by free-text query. Returned file_entry records include the content field when available, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.

search_files_by_filename.semantics: Search cloud files by filename. Returned file_entry records include the content field when available, so later extract steps can read that resolved field directly by handle.
